gods.name: Odin
gods.title: The One-Eyed Wanderer
gods.description: Odin is the seeker of runes, battle wisdom, prophecy, and hidden knowledge. His discoveries are powerful, but rarely without loss.

image.alt: Odin seated in a mythic hall

gods.name: Thor
gods.title: Breaker of Giants
gods.description: Thor guards the world of men with thunder, force, and Mjolnir. His stories are direct, violent, and deeply tied to the fragile border between Midgard and Jotunheim.

gods.name: Loki
gods.title: The Bound Trickster
gods.description: Loki reshapes every path he enters. His fragments reveal bargains, thefts, transformations, and the first signs of Ragnarok.

image.alt: Loki beside Idun in a Norse illustration

gods.name: Freyja
gods.title: Keeper of Seidr
gods.description: Freyja carries beauty, sorcery, desire, and battlefield selection. Her discoveries unlock seidr fragments and the cost of seeing more than mortals should.

gods.name: Tyr
gods.title: The One-Handed Oathkeeper
gods.description: Tyr stands at the threshold between courage and sacrifice. His path records the binding of Fenrir and the price of lawful oaths.

gods.name: Heimdall
gods.title: Watcher of Bifrost
gods.description: Heimdall watches the bridge between worlds and listens for the beginning of the end. His fragments track vigilance, borders, and Gjallarhorn.

gods.name: Baldr
gods.title: The Bright God
gods.description: Baldr marks the wound in the world. His death moves the library toward Helheim and makes every prophecy less avoidable.

realms.name: Asgard
realms.title: Fortress of the Aesir
realms.description: A high realm of halls, oaths, war councils, and Bifrost light. Many central god fragments begin here.

realms.name: Midgard
realms.title: The Ringed Human World
realms.description: The world of men, circled by the sea and threatened by forces from above, below, and beyond the borderlands.

image.alt: Diagram of the Norse nine worlds

realms.name: Jotunheim
realms.title: Borderland of Giants
realms.description: A hostile expanse of stone, frost, challenge, and old bloodlines. Thor and Loki both pull the explorer into its dangerous edge.

image.alt: Norse cosmology map used as realm reference

realms.name: Helheim
realms.title: The Cold Hall Below
realms.description: A low, silent realm for the dead outside heroic halls. Its discoveries are sparse, severe, and bound to Baldr's fate.

realms.name: Vanaheim
realms.title: Green Court of the Vanir
realms.description: A realm of older fertility, wealth, seidr, and fragile peace after divine war.

image.alt: Yggdrasil engraving used as Vanaheim placeholder

creatures.name: Fenrir
creatures.title: Wolf of the Final Breaking
creatures.description: Fenrir is bound by a chain made from impossibilities. His discovery path exposes oath failure, fear, and the future death of Odin.

creatures.name: Jormungandr
creatures.title: The World Serpent
creatures.description: Jormungandr circles Midgard and turns the sea into a boundary. Its fragments connect Thor, poison, and the final battle.

image.alt: Norse cosmology map used as Jormungandr placeholder

creatures.name: Sleipnir
creatures.title: Eight-Legged Wayfinder
creatures.description: Sleipnir moves between realms and carries Odin through thresholds no ordinary rider can cross.

image.alt: Odin image used for Sleipnir context

artifacts.name: Mjolnir
artifacts.title: Hammer of Returning Thunder
artifacts.description: Mjolnir protects the human world, breaks giant strength, and returns to Thor's hand as a symbol of force disciplined by duty.

image.alt: Thor image used for Mjolnir context

artifacts.name: Gungnir
artifacts.title: Spear That Does Not Miss
artifacts.description: Gungnir is tied to Odin's authority, war-binding, and the terrible certainty of vows made before battle.

image.alt: Odin image used for Gungnir context

artifacts.name: Draupnir
artifacts.title: Ring of Returning Gold
artifacts.description: Draupnir multiplies itself and links wealth, mourning, and the strange economy of divine gifts.

image.alt: Freyja image used for gold artifact context

artifacts.name: Yggdrasil
artifacts.title: Tree of Nine Worlds
artifacts.description: Yggdrasil holds the worlds in relation and turns exploration into a map of roots, wells, halls, and hidden costs.

myths.name: Ragnarok
myths.title: The Twilight Sequence
myths.description: A chain of losses, betrayals, battles, and rebirth. The Ragnarok path becomes clearer as Loki, Fenrir, Jormungandr, and Odin fragments are discovered.

myths.name: The Binding of Fenrir
myths.title: A Chain Made of Impossible Things
myths.description: The gods attempt to bind what they fear. Tyr's hand becomes the cost of a temporary order.

myths.name: The Theft of Mjolnir
myths.title: A Hammer Hidden in Giant Country
myths.description: Thor's missing hammer pulls the gods into disguise, negotiation, and violent recovery.

myths.name: The Death of Baldr
myths.title: The Mistletoe Omen
myths.description: Baldr's death turns a bright court into an omen machine. Every attempt to reverse it reveals how fragile divine order has become.

sagaFragments.title: Runes Do Not Answer Twice
sagaFragments.description: The first mark gives direction. The second mark demands memory. The third takes a name from the speaker.

image.alt: Yggdrasil engraving as rune fragment image

sagaFragments.title: Frost Map of the Giant Road
sagaFragments.description: A route appears only when breath freezes over the vellum. The path bends away from warm hands.

image.alt: Norse nine worlds map as frost map image

sagaFragments.title: Gate Below the Root
sagaFragments.description: No hinge moves. No guard speaks. The gate opens only when the living stop bargaining with the dead.

recommendations.title: Follow Odin's rune path
recommendations.description: Recommended because the explorer has already discovered Yggdrasil and Ragnarok. This path deepens rune and prophecy content.

recommendations.title: Complete the Fenrir sequence
recommendations.description: Recommended for explorers who have discovered Fenrir or Tyr-related fragments and can handle danger level 9 content.

recommendations.title: Open the Baldr-Helheim trail
recommendations.description: Recommended after Helheim becomes visible in the realm library. This unlocks tragic saga fragments and lower-world entries.

userDiscoveries.notes: Realm hub opened for Aesir paths.

explorationActivity.action: opened Helheim as a visible realm
